t_ch: 你别赖别人，你不也被我打败了吗？
t_en: Hey, stop blaming others! Didn't you lose to me, too?

t_ch: 难道说，我又要…我不！我不我不我不！凭什么，仙舟的小铁片子就能击败公司的无敌机甲！肯、肯定是你们作弊了，这一场不算，我要求重来！
t_en: So, does that mean... I... I have to...? No way! This can't be happening! How could a tiny piece of scrap metal defeat the IPC's mechs!? You guys must have cheated! This doesn't count! I demand a rematch!

t_ch: 小铁片子、小铁片子…真正决定胜利的可从来不是什么武器，是人！
t_en: Scrap metal, huh?... The key to victory is never the weapon. It's the person wielding it!

t_ch: 在那之前，按照老规矩……
t_en: But let's not forget about our bet...

t_ch: 老规矩，别忘了！
t_en: Yeah, let's not forget our bet!

t_ch: 金人巷的父老乡亲都看着呢。
t_en: Everyone in Aurum Alley is watching.

t_ch: 就是就是，都是老熟人了。
t_en: Right! I bet you're quite familiar with them, too.

t_ch: 我其实很佩服你愿赌服输。
t_en: Actually, I respect that you're willing to honor the bet.

t_ch: 你要是说到做到，咱也佩服你。
t_en: I'll respect you too if you keep your word.

t_ch: 够了——也不是第一次了，我道歉！
t_en: Enough...! Fine, I've done this before anyway! I apologize!

t_ch: 我要向不夜侯的小老板娘道歉，是我惹事生非、故意找事，全都怪我！
t_en: I want to apologize to the owner of the Sleepless Earl! It's my fault for causing trouble and making a scene on purpose!

t_ch: 你还没学猪叫呢？
t_en: What about the "squealing like a pig" part?

t_ch: 对啊，猪叫呢！
t_en: Yeah, don't forget the squealing!

t_ch: （适当地沉默，暗示他）
t_en: (Stay silent, hinting at him)

t_ch: 你刚才怎么说的，学猪叫呢？
t_en: Hey, remember what you said? What about squealing like a pig?

t_ch: 对啊，对啊。
t_en: Make him squeal, make him squeal!

t_ch: 等一下，我要录下来。
t_en: Hold on a sec, I wanna get this on video.

t_ch: 此时此刻，恰如彼时彼刻…在学猪叫之前，我还有一句话要说！
t_en: It's happening all over again... Alright! But before I squeal like a pig, there's something else I want to say!

t_ch: 公司机甲，猪头才要——
t_en: IPC mechs are a total joke...!

t_ch: 哼哧！呼噜呼噜！哼——
t_en: Oink! Oink, oink! Oink...

t_ch: 哼哧哼哧呼噜噜——
t_en: Oink, oink, oink...!

t_ch: 斯科特专员垂头丧气地离开了码头。
t_en: Skott leaves the dock with his head hanging low.

t_ch: 一段小插曲结束了，而三月七的剑术学习还将继续。
t_en: This brief interlude has concluded, yet March 7th's pursuit of the art of swordplay shall continue.

t_ch: 三月，你在写什么呢？
t_en: What are you writing, March?

t_ch: 我在给星穹列车写信。出来这么久了，也该报个平安才对。仙舟不是有首诗嘛！「烽火连三月，家书抵万金」！
t_en: I'm writing a letter to the Astral Express. It's been a while, and I thought I should let them know that I'm safe and sound here. You know that poem from the Xianzhou, right? "We like March. Her shoes are purple, she is new and high..."

t_ch: 你这个引用疑似是有点不吉利了……
t_en: March, I don't think that poem is about you...

t_ch: 姬子、杨叔、帕姆：见信如晤。我们在罗浮一切都好，请各位放心。你们的旅行还顺利吧？
t_en: Dear Himeko, Mr. Yang, and Pom-Pom, We're all good here on the Xianzhou Luofu, so no need to worry! By the way, how's your trip going?

t_ch: 协助三月七练习剑术
t_en: Help March 7th with her sword training.

t_ch: 我先去休息一下，告辞。
t_en: I am going to rest for a while, farewell.

t_ch: 在下听闻阁下已经为剑斗做足了准备，祝阁下宝剑常锋，登擂夺胜！
t_en: I hear that you have already made sufficient battle preparations. May thy blade split and sever, and may victory find you!

t_ch: 三月七小姐、{NICKNAME}，听说斯科特已经送来了战帖，我们想着能不能做点什么，帮助三月小姐战胜斯科特。
t_en: March, {NICKNAME}, we heard that Skott sent you a challenge, so we were wondering if there's anything we can do to help you defeat him.

t_ch: 谢谢藿藿，你愿意教咱学会摆弄除魔道具，就已经帮了我很多了！多亏了各位相助，本姑娘虽然学剑没有多长时间，但击败公司的机甲我还是有信心的。
t_en: Thanks, Huohuo! You've already been a huge help by teaching me how to use the exorcising tools! Even though I've only been learning swordplay for a short time, I'm confident I can take down the IPC mechs.

t_ch: 哪里哪里，是我要多感谢三月七小姐才是。
t_en: Don't mention it. It's actually me who should be thanking you, March.

t_ch: 我记得那个斯科特不是被{NICKNAME}教训过一次了吗？怎么还敢回来？人类是学不会吸取教训嘛？
t_en: But seriously, didn't {NICKNAME} kick Skott's butt already? So why's he back again? Don't humans ever learn from their mistakes?

t_ch: 尾巴大爷此言差矣，有句话叫「君子报仇十年不晚」。但是斯科特是个小人，我猜他连隔夜仇都忍不了，现在可让他逮着机会了。
t_en: No, no, Mr. Tail. There's a saying, "Revenge is a dish best served cold," but Skott hardly has any patience. He couldn't resist the temptation to get back at us.

t_ch: 不过嘛，要是就这样击败他…总觉得有一些…不够痛快！
t_en: Still, if that's how I beat him... it'd be a little less... satisfying.

t_ch: 狠狠击败斯科特也不够痛快？
t_en: Giving Skott a good beating isn't satisfying enough for you?

t_ch: 唔…说不出来，就是感觉不够痛快。
t_en: Hmm... I can't quite put my finger on it, but it just wouldn't be satisfying enough.

t_ch: 怎么样才算是足够痛快呢？
t_en: So, what would be your ideal way of teaching him a lesson?

t_ch: 我也说不清楚……
t_en: Actually, I have no idea...

t_ch: 我明白。这种学不会吸取教训的家伙，就算毒打他千遍万遍，也还是不会吸取任何教训。他是不会发自内心被你打服的。
t_en: I get it. Some people never learn, no matter how many times you knock them down. They'll never admit defeat or learn anything.

t_ch: 那…有什么办法，能让他输得心服口服？
t_en: So... how do we get him to admit defeat?

t_ch: 尾巴大爷有什么好办法？
t_en: Do you have any ideas, Mr. Tail?

t_ch: 不是本大爷说你呀，你太顺着那个斯科特的思路走了。
t_en: Listen kid, I'm not trying to rag on you, but you're always playing into Skott's hands with your approach.

t_ch: 他订下规则用机甲与你手中三尺剑比斗，你就同意了。他说赢了怎样、输了怎样，你也同意了…这整个儿就是一场斯科特组织的运动会啊，赢了也没劲！
t_en: He set up these rules for a fight between his mechs and your sword, and you agreed. Then he laid out what to do if you win or lose, and you agreed again... It's basically just another one of Skott's organized sports events, and it's not even fun if you win!

t_ch: 规则就是用来打破的！
t_en: Rules are made to be broken!

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#你小子}，别动不动耍帅。这样的机会要好好留给本大爷。
t_en: Hey, cut it out kid, just leave the cool catchphrases to me!

t_ch: 尾巴大爷有何高见呢？
t_en: What do you think, Mr. Tail?

t_ch: 哼哼，你算是问对人了。
t_en: Hmm, looks like you've come to the right person.

t_ch: 咱是岁阳，你要是信得过我的话，大爷我有些岁阳的本事！藿藿，揭开封印吧……
t_en: I'm a heliobus, and I've got a few tricks up my sleeve! Just remove my seal, Huohuo...

t_ch: 揭开封印没关系吗？
t_en: Is it really okay to remove your seal?

t_ch: 能有什么关系？只要你不给女鬼判官打小报告就行。
t_en: What's the harm? As long as you don't go snitching to that Ghosty, we won't run into any trouble.

t_ch: 你打算去附身斯科特？
t_en: Are you planning to possess Skott?

t_ch: 嘿嘿嘿，只是附身可是不够的，我要深入他的内心。
t_en: Heh, just possessing him won't be enough. I'm going to get inside his head.

t_ch: 你们等着瞧吧，本大爷会让斯科特洗心革面，诚心诚意地认输，你说这痛快不痛快！
t_en: Just wait and see. I'll make Skott change his mind and admit defeat wholeheartedly. What do you say?

t_ch: 喔！不愧是尾巴大爷！
t_en: Whoa! You're awesome, Mr. Tail!

t_ch: 就拜托你了，尾巴大爷！
t_en: I'll leave it to you then, Mr. Tail!

t_ch: 稍等，我还有些事情。
t_en: Just a moment. I have something else to do.

t_ch: 斯科特专员！斯科特专员！你怎么了？
t_en: Specialist Skott! Specialist Skott! What's the matter?
Whew...

t_ch: 斯科特专员，刚才三月七一挥剑，就有一个绿色的东西冲进了你身体里…然后你就站在那里，嘴上念念有词…你还好吗？
t_en: Specialist Skott, March 7th swung her sword, and something green entered your body... Then you froze and started muttering something... Are you okay?

t_ch: 喂！三月七，你们到底对斯科特专员做了些什么！
t_en: Hey! March 7th! What did you do to Specialist Skott!?

t_ch: 我？我什么也没做啊，可别怪到我身上。
t_en: Me? I didn't do anything, seriously.

t_ch: 尾巴大爷，你…你对斯科特做了什么？
t_en: Mr. Tail, what... what did you do to Skott?

t_ch: 你不会把他的情绪吸干了吧？
t_en: You didn't drain all of his emotions, did you?

t_ch: 你不会是把他给夺舍了吧？
t_en: You didn't possess him, did you?

t_ch: 想什么呢？本大爷只是抢走他内心最扭曲的欲望而已，你们就瞧好吧，哈哈！
t_en: Come on, what are you thinking? I just stripped away his most twisted desire! Watch what's about to happen, haha!

t_ch: 呜呜，呜……
t_en: Wooh, woooh...

t_ch: 斯科特的样子看起来怪怪的…尾巴大爷，你真让他洗心革面了？
t_en: Skott seems weird... Did you really make him change his ways, Mr. Tail?

t_ch: 老子也是第一次尝试，还不知道效果如何。
t_en: Well, this is my first time doing something like this, so I'm not sure how things will turn out.

t_ch: 能让这种家伙洗心革面，尾巴大爷，你可真不简单啊。
t_en: You've managed to make a guy like him turn over a new leaf. You're amazing, Mr. Tail!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，本大爷比你们想的更不简单。快看，开始了——
t_en: Heh, I'm even more amazing than you think. Look, it's kicking in now...

t_ch: 嗷！呜！嗷呜——
t_en: Hawoo! Hawooo...!

t_ch: 斯科特专员，你怎么了！
t_en: Specialist Skott, what's wrong with you!?

t_ch: 嗷嗷呜！呜——
t_en: Owoo! Hohohohohow awoo...

t_ch: 嗷呜！嗷呜！嗷——
t_en: Howoo! Howoo! Awoo...

t_ch: 斯科特专员，你清醒一点啊！
t_en: Specialist Skott, snap out of it!

t_ch: 尾巴，你到底把他怎么了？
t_en: What have you done to him, Mr. Tail?

t_ch: 没有了那扭曲的欲望——他是真心诚意地想成为一匹「孤狼」了。
t_en: I removed his twisted desires, and now he's genuinely trying to become a "lone wolf."

t_ch: 你真厉害。
t_en: You're incredible.

t_ch: 那自然厉害，老子可是抢走了他扭曲的内心！
t_en: Of course I am. I've stolen his twisted heart!

t_ch: 幸好我们是友军。
t_en: Good thing you're on our side.

t_ch: 那你可记好了，千万不要与藿藿为敌哦。
t_en: Yeah, you'd ought to remember it's best not to make an enemy out of Huohuo.

t_ch: 这也算是「洗心革面」？
t_en: Is this what you call "changing his ways?"

t_ch: 怎么不算呢？野生动物不是比这家伙善良多了？
t_en: What would you call it, then? Even a feral wolf is better than what he was, right?

t_ch: 嗷！嗷呜——
t_en: Awoo! Grrr...!

t_ch: 啊！斯科特专员，你别咬我！
t_en: Ah! Don't bite me, Specialist Skott!

t_ch: 嗷呜！嗷嗷嗷——
t_en: Awooo! Howhowhowooooo...!

t_ch: 快跑啊！斯科特专员发狂咬人了！
t_en: Run for your life! Specialist Skott has gone insane!

t_ch: 几个公司员工拔腿就跑，斯科特专员在身后手脚并用、穷追不舍。真是残酷的动物世界。
t_en: Several IPC workers take off immediately, and Skott pursues them on all fours.

t_ch: 我还以为尾巴大爷会附在我身上，引导我展现出超凡绝伦的岁阳剑法呢……
t_en: I thought Mr. Tail would possess me and teach me some super cool heliobus swordplay...

t_ch: 都说了，本大爷不是那么暴力的岁阳！
t_en: Nah, I told you I'm not really one for violence!

t_ch: 一段小插曲结束了，而三月七的剑术学习还将继续。
t_en: This brief interlude has concluded, yet March 7th's pursuit of the art of swordplay shall continue.

t_ch: 三月小姐…你在写什么呢？
t_en: What are you writing... Miss March?

t_ch: 我在给星穹列车写信。出来这么久了，也该报个平安才对。
t_en: I'm writing a letter to the Astral Express. It's been a while, and I thought I should let them know that I'm safe and sound here.

t_ch: 哇，你的字好好看啊！那个…能不能抽空也教教尾巴大爷？
t_en: Wow, your handwriting is so beautiful! Can you... teach Mr. Tail sometime?

t_ch: 嘿！这小东西的胆子是越来越大了，敢讽刺你尾巴大爷了是吧？
t_en: Hey! This little one is getting cheeky! Now you're being sarcastic with me, huh?

t_ch: 姬子、杨叔、帕姆：见信如晤。我们在罗浮一切都好，请各位放心。你们的旅行还顺利吧？
t_en: Dear Himeko, Mr. Yang, and Pom-Pom, We're all good here on the Xianzhou Luofu, so no need to worry! By the way, how's your trip going?

t_ch: 你们等着瞧吧，本大爷会让斯科特洗心革面，诚心诚意地认输，你说这痛快不痛快！
t_en: Just wait and see. I'll make Skott change his mind and admit defeat wholeheartedly. What do you say?

t_ch: 喔！不愧是尾巴大爷！
t_en: Whoa! You're awesome, Mr. Tail!

t_ch: 就拜托你了，尾巴大爷！
t_en: I'll leave it to you then, Mr. Tail!

t_ch: 稍等，我还有些事情。
t_en: Just a moment. I have something else to do.

t_ch: 好久不见，{NICKNAME}，三月七小姐！
t_en: Long time no see, {NICKNAME} and Miss March 7th!

t_ch: 好久不见，最近捉鬼还忙吗？
t_en: Long time, still busy busting ghosts recently?

t_ch: 跟岁阳相关的事务没之前那么多了，可是别的事情又开始忙起来了……
t_en: There are fewer heliobus-related jobs than before, though they've been replaced with other matters...

t_ch: 藿藿，快救救被附身的三月七！
t_en: Huohuo, hurry up and save the possessed March 7th!

t_ch: 啊、啊啊啊！三月小姐坚持住，我这就把灵符拿出来…！
t_en: Ah, ahhhh! Hang in there Miss March, I'll get the talisman out right now...!

t_ch: 别担心藿藿，我没有被附身，{F#她}{M#他}在逗你呢。
t_en: Don't worry Huohuo, I'm not possessed, {F#she's}{M#he's} just pulling your leg.

t_ch: 好久不见！你在这里做什么呢？
t_en: It's been a while! What're you doing here?

t_ch: 我、我在星槎海休息。在这里看着星槎一艘一艘过来过去，很解压的。
t_en: I—I'm taking a break at Starskiff Haven. Just watching the starskiffs sail one by one, it's really relaxing.

t_ch: 看来十王司的判官压力也很大呀。
t_en: Being a Ten-Lords Commission judge is pretty stressful too, I bet.

t_ch: 也很大…？三月七小姐最近遇上了什么压力很大的事情了吗？
t_en: Too...? Have you run into something that's been weighing heavily on your mind, Miss March 7th?

t_ch: 最近我开始学习仙舟剑法了，有了一些学业压力，以及…社会压力。
t_en: I've started picking up Xianzhou swordplay, so there's been some academic stress, and... social stress.

t_ch: 你将三月七与公司的好事之徒约战的事细细道来。
t_en: You elaborate on the matter of the upcoming duel between March 7th and that IPC busybody.

t_ch: 事、事情我大概明白了，想要在短时间内掌握一门剑术并不容易，三月七小姐有压力也不奇怪。我、我会一些驱邪的剑术，如果你有需要的话……
t_en: I—I think I get the gist of it. It's not easy trying to master a school's swordplay in such a short time, no wonder Miss March 7th is stressed out. I know some evil-warding swordplay, so if you need some help...

t_ch: 算了吧，你连剑都握不稳，就这水平还想帮别人？
t_en: Hah, you can't even hold your sword steady. Don't you think you're overestimating your skills?

t_ch: …我、我是握不稳。你不是握都握不了吗？
t_en: ...S—Sure, I can't hold it steady. But I'm better than you — you don't even have hands!

t_ch: 哎哟…小怂包还敢顶嘴了。要是老子附在你身上，你登上演武仪典的擂台，绝对不会输给神策府里那个黄毛小子！
t_en: Ooh... The timid fry's got a sharp tongue. If you were in the Wardance ring and I possessed you, you'd have to try really hard to lose to that brat from the Seat of Divine Foresight!

t_ch: 彦卿师父他真的很厉害，尾巴大爷不会是吹牛吧？
t_en: Master Yanqing is really powerful. You sure you aren't just blowing smoke, Mr. Tail?

t_ch: 哼，老子只要附身过剑技高超之人，汲取那人的记忆与情绪，本大爷就能掌握相同的剑技！你要是想学那个黄毛小子的剑法，这不是最快的？
t_en: Hmph, as long as I possess someone of extraordinary caliber and absorb that person's memories and emotions, I can master the exact same technique! Isn't that the fastest way to do it if you want to learn that brat's swordplay?

t_ch: 不行不行，我是不会解开封印的！
t_en: No no no, I won't undo the seal!

t_ch: 啧啧啧，看来你想帮朋友的心意也不过如此嘛？
t_en: Tsk tsk, I guess your desire to help your friend is only worth this much, eh?

t_ch: 二位的心意谢过了。{NICKNAME}，我想听听你对「走捷径」的看法？
t_en: Thank you both for looking out for me. {NICKNAME}, I wonder what you think about this shortcut?

t_ch: 时间很紧，借个存档倒不错。
t_en: Time is tight, it's a good idea to borrow a save file.

t_ch: 存、存档？这么一说确实很像是问某个高端玩家借了存档。不过嘛，等尾巴大爷一走，我这边还不是一无所获嘛。
t_en: S—Save file? When you put it this way, it does sound like asking to borrow a hardcore player's save file. But once Mr. Tail leaves, I'd still be left empty-handed.

t_ch: 学什么都不能走捷径！
t_en: There are no shortcuts when it comes to learning!

t_ch: 不愧是咱的助教，你说的不错，要是尾巴大爷一走，我一无所获，这可就违背了学剑的初衷了。
t_en: That's our tutor for you! You're spot on, when Mr. Tail leaves me, I'll be left with nothing. That goes against the fundamentals of swordplay training.

t_ch: 三月小姐也觉得这样不好，对吧？
t_en: You also think it's a bad idea, right Miss March?

t_ch: 呸呸呸，好心当成了驴肝肺，你想要，老子还不愿帮呢！
t_en: Bah, and here I was trying to be nice! Even if you wanted it, I wouldn't help you now!

t_ch: …啊！刚刚寒鸦大人发来了一例「僵尸」的案情，看来休息时间结束了。三月小姐，我们下次再见吧！
t_en: ...Ah! Madam Hanya just sent over a zombie case, looks like break time's over. We'll talk again, Miss March!

t_ch: 再见！
t_en: Catch you later!

t_ch: 藿藿急匆匆地离开了……
t_en: Huohuo scuttles off in a hurry...

t_ch: 仔细想了想，尾巴大爷的提议倒也不是没有可取之处。<color=#dbc291ff>下次遇见藿藿时，再向她和尾巴大爷请教一番好了。</color>
t_en: After giving it some thought, I think there's something to be gleaned from Mr. Tail's suggestion. When I <color=#dbc291ff>see Huohuo again, I should ask for advice from both her and Mr. Tail.</color>

t_ch: 啊…是{NICKNAME}，还有三月七小姐！
t_en: Ah... It's {NICKNAME}, and Miss March 7th too!

t_ch: 藿藿，怎么没有精神的样子？
t_en: Huohuo, why do you look so dazed?

t_ch: 嗯，最近我们在绥园附近发现了两具「僵尸」。说是「僵尸」也不太对…受害者身上似乎没有岁阳留存，也没有明显的外伤，但却会重复做出某些奇怪的行为。
t_en: Mm, we found two zombies near Fyxestroll Garden recently. Well, "zombie" might not be the right word... The victims had no heliobus traces on them, nor obvious external injuries, but they kept repeating some strange behavior.

t_ch: 有点意思！
t_en: How interesting!

t_ch: 三月七小姐，就别挖苦我了。
t_en: Miss March 7th, please don't patronize me...

t_ch: 肯定是惊吓过度的反应。
t_en: Must be a side effect from being scared silly.

t_ch: …也许有可能！不过，我有没有做过像这样的奇怪行为呢？
t_en: ...That's entirely possible! But have I ever acted so strangely?

t_ch: 你跑得老快了，这事应该没这么简单。
t_en: You run super fast. This matter's probably not that simple.

t_ch: 这只能说明那是赛博僵尸。
t_en: That could only mean that it's a cyber-zombie.

t_ch: 不是所有东西前面加上「赛博」两个字就会合理的。
t_en: Not everything will suddenly make sense just because you tack the word "cyber" in front of it.

t_ch: 毕竟人还活着，说是「僵尸」有点太不礼貌了。
t_en: The person's still alive, so it's not very nice to call them a zombie.

t_ch: 就像你和{NICKNAME}组成「捉鬼小队」时那样，我们一起来分析一下吧！下面有请藿专家介绍案情。
t_en: Just like when you and {NICKNAME} formed the ghost-hunting squad, let's analyze this together! Next up, let's have resident expert Huohuo introduce the case.

t_ch: 好、好，跟大家一起商量一下，可能更容易打开思路！
t_en: Okay, okay. Something might click if we put our brains together!

t_ch: 第一位受害者，是演武仪典的报名选手。被发现的时候肌肉僵硬，双手前举，嘴里念念有词各种功法口诀。
t_en: The first victim is a Wardance combat registrant. Their muscles were rigid when they were discovered, their arms were stretched out in front of them, and they were muttering all sorts of exercise rhymes.

t_ch: 第二位受害者，他也是演武仪典的报名选手。受害人是前云骑军成员，擅使长枪，被发现时正拿长枪练习撑杆跳。
t_en: The second victim is also a Wardance combat registrant. He is a former Cloud Knight proficient at the spear. He was discovered practicing pole-vaulting with his spear.

t_ch: 唔，受害者有一个共通点——他们都是演武仪典的选手。看起来凶手专挑演武仪典的选手作为目标！
t_en: Mm, the victims have something in common — they're both Wardance contestants. It appears the culprit is expressly choosing Wardance contestants as their targets!

t_ch: 三月七，你也是参赛选手。
t_en: March 7th, you're a participant too.

t_ch: 所以呢？本姑娘会成为下一个目标吗？
t_en: So? I'll be the next target then?

t_ch: 在受害者身上找共通点是误区。
t_en: It's a mistake to look for similarities on the victims.

t_ch: 没错，受害者太少了，这共通点没有说服力。
t_en: That's right, we have too few victims. This similarity isn't convincing enough.

t_ch: 你就别费力一通分析了，让咱们把情况说全乎些。
t_en: You should give the analysis a rest first and let us finish talking.

t_ch: 除去都是参赛选手外，这两个受害人还有一个共同的特征——他们都是看了罗浮杂俎上的流言，跑到绥园附近瞎逛的傻瓜。
t_en: Besides the fact that they were participants, they also had a similar characteristic — they're both fools who went to Fyxestroll Garden to poke around after reading the messages on the Luofu's Ghostly Grove.

t_ch: 这两名受害者相信绥园凌厉肃杀的阴气能磨砺他们的招式，于是趁着开赛前在这儿练功，想要吸取天地之气为己用——都什么年代了怎么还有人信这？
t_en: These victims believed that Fyxestroll Garden's vengeful gloom would temper their skills, and decided to train here before their matches started, in some attempt to harness the worldly energies absorbed here... People still believe this mumbo-jumbo in this day and age?

t_ch: 最后，此二人都被值勤的十王司冥差「九叔」劝返回家，于次日发作异常。
t_en: Finally, these two were shooed home by the Ten-Lords Commission spiritfarer-on-duty, Uncle Jiu. They started acting funny the following day.

t_ch: 破案了，九叔是凶手！
t_en: Mystery solved! Uncle Jiu is the murderer!

t_ch: 九叔是咱们的工作人员…
t_en: He's a member of our staff...

t_ch: 破案了，肯定是岁阳！
t_en: Mystery solved! It must be a heliobus!

t_ch: 我猜八成也是逃逸的岁阳搞的鬼。
t_en: I'm also guessing it's an escaped heliobus that's up to no good.

t_ch: 好像在仙舟幻戏里看过相同的故事！这位九叔是不是有两个徒弟，一个徒弟被僵尸咬了，还有一个徒弟被女鬼给迷住了……
t_en: I think I've seen a similar story in a Xianzhou immersia! Does this Uncle Jiu have two disciples, and one of them got bitten by a zombie, and the other got charmed by a female ghost...

t_ch: 咱们这位九叔…没有徒弟。
t_en: Our Uncle Jiu... has no disciples.

t_ch: 你少看点幻戏，多接触点现实吧！
t_en: Cut down on your immersia addiction and touch some grass!

t_ch: 不过三月七小姐既然要参加演武仪典，一定要千万小心，发生什么事记得联系我哦！
t_en: But since you want to join the Wardance, you must be careful! Don't forget to contact me if anything happens!

t_ch: 加油藿藿，早点将犯人捉拿归案！
t_en: Good luck, Huohuo! Hope you catch the culprit and close the case soon!

t_ch: 两位再见！
t_en: Goodbye!

t_ch: 藿藿急匆匆地离开了……
t_en: Huohuo scuttles off in a hurry...

t_ch: 如果那只岁阳专挑演武仪典的选手做目标…{NICKNAME}，咱们不如在有空的时候去绥园碰碰运气，也许能帮到藿藿也说不定呢？
t_en: If that heliobus targets Wardance contenders on purpose... {NICKNAME}, how about we try our luck at Fyxestroll Garden when we've got time? We might be able to help Huohuo.

t_ch: 哇，不得了不得了，你有道灵光从天灵盖喷出来。你年纪轻轻就有一身横练的筋骨，简直是百年一见的练武奇才啊！
t_en: Woah! This is amazing, you've got a spiritual light gushing out of your crown. To have the physique of a martial prodigy at such a young age, you're a martial genius who only appears once in a century!

t_ch: 小心，是逃逸的岁阳！
t_en: Careful, it's that escaped heliobus!

t_ch: 嗯，本姑娘小心提防着呢！
t_en: Okay, I'm on high alert right now!

t_ch: 你别再夸三月七了，她真会信。
t_en: You should stop lavishing your praises on March 7th, she might actually believe you.

t_ch: 什么意思嘛，我本来就是练武的奇才。
t_en: What'd you mean, huh? I'm a born martial genius.

t_ch: 只要让我沾着你的身子，便立时能打通你的任督二脉，让你凭空增长百年功力。在演武仪典上获胜也不过是小菜一碟！
t_en: As long as you let me attach on to you, your core meridians will be instantly activated, allowing you to attain a century's worth of effort in no time. Victory at the Wardance will be a cakewalk!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，想利用本姑娘的胜负心是吧？那可不行！藏头露尾的家伙，快给我出来！
t_en: Heh-heh, trying to appeal to my competitive spirit? No can do! Stop cowering in the shadows and show yourself!

t_ch: 好啦，被你发现了。说真的，我精通天下各门各派的武艺，让我摸摸你的脑袋，一眨眼就可以全数传授给你喔。
t_en: Fine, you got me. Honestly though, I'm a master of all the martial arts across all schools. Just let me touch your head, and I'll be able to impart all my skills to you in the blink of an eye.

t_ch: 代价是什么呢？该不会是变成僵尸，只能口吐歪比巴比之类的胡话吧？算了，我不想学。
t_en: At what price? I don't suppose it's becoming a zombie and sputtering gibberish, is it? Forget it, I'm not learning any of that.

t_ch: 不想学？…那可由不得你了！我免费授课，你不学也得学！
t_en: You don't want to?... I'm afraid that's not up to you! I give my lessons free of charge, you have no choice but to learn them!

t_ch: 三月七只觉眼前一黑，数不尽的拳脚功夫在一瞬间涌入脑海。
t_en: All March 7th can see before her is darkness, countless martial arts of myriad unarmed styles all surging into her mind.

t_ch: 可惜，其中没有一招和剑术有关。
t_en: Unfortunately, though, not a single move is related to swordplay.
Ahhhh!!!

t_ch: 别怕！三月小姐，我来了！
t_en: Fear not, Miss March! Here I come!

t_ch: 我、我差一点就来晚了。
t_en: I—I was almost too late.

t_ch: 多亏了藿藿，刚才那个好为人师的岁阳已经被吸收走了吗？
t_en: Thank goodness you were here, Huohuo. Has that overly enthusiastic heliobus been subdued?

t_ch: 嗯嗯，我已经将流窜的岁阳收进藏月瓠里了。
t_en: Yes, I've subdued that vagrant heliobus in the Lunaumbra Gourd.

t_ch: 可恶，手脚突然动弹不得……
t_en: Ugh, I suddenly can't move my arms and legs...

t_ch: 我给你贴上符纸，你再好好休息一会……
t_en: I'll apply a talisman on you, and then you need to have a good rest...

t_ch: 一段时间后……
t_en: Some time later...

t_ch: 看来，现在我已经彻底理解「僵尸」之谜了——这岁阳专门利用参赛选手的好胜心理，将什么武艺口诀一股脑地灌进了人的思维之中。
t_en: I think I've gotten to the bottom of this zombie mystery. This heliobus preyed on the competitive will of contenders to channel all its martial mantras into their minds.

t_ch: 如何抬手、如何抬脚，如何移动身体，无限制的信息不断涌入脑海之中…可惜人和岁阳不一样，海量的信息只会让人大脑过载而呆立原地、动弹不得。
t_en: How to raise your arms and legs, how to move your body, infinite information surging into your mind non-stop... A pity we're not like heliobi — such a massive magnitude of information will only overload our minds and render us immobile.

t_ch: 身体已经跟不上脑子了！
t_en: The body can't keep up with the mind!

t_ch: 虽然时间很短暂，我好像感觉什么都能看到，什么都能听到，却什么也做不到。
t_en: While it was just for a short time, I felt like I could see everything, hear everything, but I couldn't do a single thing.

t_ch: 原来这就是身体跟不上脑子了吗？
t_en: So this is what it means when the body can't keep up with the mind?

t_ch: 学习并不是一个单向过程。
t_en: The path of learning isn't a one-way street.

t_ch: 我已经切身体会到了，刚才那只能算是精神冲击，根本就不是学习。
t_en: I've personally learned that painful lesson. What happened was not learning at all, it was nothing more than sheer mind shock.

t_ch: 既然三月七小姐恢复得差不多了，我也该回去封印岁阳了，两位，我们下次再见！
t_en: Now that you've recovered, I should be getting back to seal this heliobus. We'll meet again!

t_ch: 再见藿藿！
t_en: Goodbye Huohuo!

t_ch: 藿藿急匆匆地离开了……
t_en: Huohuo scuttles off in a hurry...

t_ch: 今天本来是想和藿藿一起打发点时间的，不过她有自己的事要办，还是等下次见面再说吧。
t_en: I was thinking about spending some time with Huohuo today, but she's got stuff to do. Let's chat again next time.

t_ch: 被岁阳一附身就好累啊，{NICKNAME}，咱们去吃点好吃的吧。
t_en: It's draining being possessed by a heliobus, {NICKNAME}. Let's go grab something tasty to eat.

t_ch: 为了帮助藿藿解决「僵尸谜案」，你与三月七来到夜半的绥园之中。
t_en: To help Huohuo resolve the "zombie case," you and March 7th arrived in Fyxestroll Garden in the middle of the night.

t_ch: 岁阳专挑演武仪典的选手袭击，不出意外，你们遇上了不速之客……
t_en: The heliobus is focused specifically on attacking Wardance contestants. As expected, you encountered an undesired guest...

t_ch: 十王司已经封印了那个好为人师的岁阳，它已经再也没有办法四处害人了。而之前受到它影响的演武仪典参赛选手也逐渐恢复了意识。
t_en: The Ten-Lords Commission has sealed away that heliobus who's always eager to act as a tutor. It won't be harming anyone anymore. The Wardance participants who fell to its antics are also slowly recovering their senses.

t_ch: 这次小小的岁阳之乱也算是平息了。这都多亏了三月七小姐，还有{NICKNAME}。
t_en: It's all thanks to you and {NICKNAME} that this mini Heliobi Wars has been quelled.

t_ch: 也要多亏了藿藿，不愧是十王司的判官。
t_en: Thanks to you too, Huohuo. No wonder you're a Ten-Lords Commission Judge.

t_ch: 上次三月七小姐说有话要说……
t_en: You had something you wished to say last time...

t_ch: 其实之前我听说尾巴大爷说，能够将「学剑」的记忆直接灌输给我的时候，我必须承认，我当时有那么一点点心动，当然也只是一点点而已——
t_en: It's about when Mr. Tail said that the memories of swordplay practice could be channeled into me — I have to admit that I was the teeniest bit tempted. Just a tiny bit!

t_ch: 老子提出的建议不错吧。
t_en: My idea's pretty decent, eh?

t_ch: 当时我想要让尾巴大爷帮忙，让我学个一招半式的，不过嘛，有些难以启齿……
t_en: I wanted to have Mr. Tail help me out then, to at least learn a move or two. But I was too embarrassed to admit it...

t_ch: 没人能抵挡「走捷径」的诱惑。
t_en: No one can withstand the temptation of a shortcut.

t_ch: 你这么说不太对，毕竟我最后应该算是抵挡住了诱惑。
t_en: That's not really true. I mean, I still resisted it in the end.

t_ch: 你还是拒绝了「工业化学剑」。
t_en: You also rejected "industrial learning."

t_ch: 你要是能少创造一些这类耳目一新的词语就好了。
t_en: It'd be great if you could stop coming up with such fresh new terms.

t_ch: 幸好藿藿比较忙，没让你得逞。
t_en: Luckily Huohuo was too busy to let you have your way.

t_ch: 你其实可以将这句话换成「三月七的定力远超常人」的。
t_en: You can rephrase that to "March 7th's willpower far surpasses ordinary people."

t_ch: 要不是遇上了上次的岁阳，我不会意识到「拥有剑法的知识」和「掌握剑法」之间还是隔了太多太多，那不是学习剑法的捷径。
t_en: If I hadn't run into that heliobus, I wouldn't have understood the sheer distance between my knowledge and mastery of swordplay. That's not a shortcut to learning swordplay at all.

t_ch: 这一点我很赞同三月七小姐。
t_en: I very much agree with Miss March 7th on this.

t_ch: 不过，老子听说忆庭使者有着比咱们岁阳更加柔和、更加安全的手段，将原本不存在的「剑法记忆」塞入脑海中…如果是这样，你还会拒绝吗？
t_en: Though I've heard of a much milder and safer method that the Garden's messengers have, that can shove non-existent swordplay memories into your mind... In that case, would you still refuse?

t_ch: 我可能还是会拒绝吧。
t_en: I might still have to decline.

t_ch: 我想，只有用自己的脑子去思考、用自己的身体去体会，才能把「彦卿师父的剑」、「云璃师父的剑」最后变成「三月七的剑」。
t_en: I think that I have to put my own thought into it, and experience it myself to eventually turn "Master Yanqing's Sword" and "Master Yunli's Sword" into "March 7th's Sword."

t_ch: 老子承认你说的有点道理。
t_en: You make a good point there, girlie.

t_ch: 如果这个过程变成了复制粘贴，我永远都超越不了两位小师父。
t_en: If my journey was nothing more than a copy-and-paste process, I'd never be able to surpass the two masters.

t_ch: 感觉你这一趟确实领悟了不少。
t_en: Sounds like you've had quite the epiphany.

t_ch: 通常来说，我讲出「富含哲理的话」，就应该代表着我的剑术造诣又提升了吧？
t_en: For me to be waxing philosophical pearls of wisdom, that should mean I've attained new heights with my swordplay, am I right?

t_ch: 会说几句鸡汤就算提升了？既然你想得这么通透，那你就好好练你的剑吧！
t_en: Spouting passages from a self-help book makes you all enlightened now? If you really think you're all that, you should be training even harder on your swordplay!

t_ch: 学习的捷径是有的，不在这里。
t_en: There is a shortcut to learning, but it's not here.

t_ch: 没错没错，能够提升学习效率的方法多的是，本姑娘可聪明了。
t_en: That's right! There are many ways to increase learning efficiency. I'm super smart!

t_ch: 这件事忙完之后，我有了一段空闲的时间，如果三月七小姐为了对付斯科特而需要帮助的话，请随时来找藿藿，我和尾巴大爷一定会帮你的。
t_en: I've got a block of free time after attending to this matter. Miss March 7th, if you need help taking on Skott, come see me anytime. Mr. Tail and I will definitely help you out.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，谢谢藿藿，谢谢尾巴大爷。
t_en: Sure, thanks Huohuo and Mr. Tail!

t_ch: 呜…我输了…连这样的训练都没办法应对，我肯定打不过斯科特……
t_en: I've lost... If I can't even handle this kind of training, there's no way I'll beat Skott...

t_ch: 看来十五天确实太短了，很难练出些名堂。
t_en: Looks like fifteen days are too short to master swordplay.

t_ch: …唉，看来只能低下头来，请斯科特放弃比武了。
t_en: ...Well, looks like we'll have to swallow our pride and ask Skott to abandon the duel.

t_ch: 如果再给我一次机会，我一定努力练剑，不会再输了！
t_en: If I get another chance, I'll practice even harder and won't ever lose again!

t_ch: 胜败乃兵家常事，请女侠重新来过。
t_en: Winning and losing are all part of the game, I encourage you to try again young hero.

t_ch: 呜…我输了……
t_en: Argh... I've lost...

t_ch: 你还好吧？
t_en: Are you alright?

t_ch: 我没受什么伤，但是……
t_en: I'm not hurt, but...

t_ch: 斯科特，你完蛋了。
t_en: Skott, you won't get away with this.

t_ch: 愿赌服输，你少威胁我。
t_en: A deal is a deal! You think I'm scared of your threats?

t_ch: 你怎么连他都打不过？
t_en: You can't even beat him?

t_ch: 都怪我学剑太不用功了！
t_en: It's all my fault! I didn't train enough!

t_ch: 彦卿师父、云璃师父，对不起…我输了……
t_en: I'm sorry, Master Yanqing, Master Yunli... I lost...

t_ch: 你不过是个只练过十五天剑术的外行罢了。能和他们战斗到这种地步，已经很了不起了。
t_en: Well, you've only been practicing swordplay for fifteen days. To even reach this point is already impressive.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，看这小妮子自信满满的样子，我还以为能有多强呢！结果还不是被我们轻松拿捏～
t_en: Hahaha, and to think she was so full of herself. I thought she'd be a tough opponent, but we handled her with ease.

t_ch: 欺负个小姑娘，值得你这么得意吗？
t_en: Do you feel proud of yourself, bullying a young girl like this?

t_ch: 彦卿、{NICKNAME}，我们仨打到他们忘掉约定为止吧。
t_en: Hey, Yanqing and {NICKNAME}, what do you say we give them a harsh beating until they forget about that stupid bet?

t_ch: 终于到我擅长的环节了。
t_en: Finally, something I'm good at.

t_ch: 这合适吗？
t_en: Do you think that's appropriate?

t_ch: 这…违反《仙舟治安管理条例》了吧？
t_en: It's probably... against the Xianzhou Public Order Regulations.

t_ch: 连弟子都不愿保护，你算什么师父？
t_en: You won't even stand up for your own apprentice? What kind of master are you?

t_ch: 喂！你们仨要干什么，玩不起就别玩啊！
t_en: Hey! What's the deal with you three? If you can't play fair, then don't play!

t_ch: 彦卿师父、云璃师父！别这样！既然我输了比武，那就要承担起相应的责任。我好歹也是个无名客，可不能留下愿赌不服输的名声。
t_en: Please stop, Master Yanqing and Master Yunli! I lost the duel, and now I have to accept the result. As a Nameless, I don't want to be known as a sore loser.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，真是痛快啊！真是痛快啊！哈哈哈哈哈哈！
t_en: Hahaha! This is so satisfying! So satisfying! Hahahahaha!

t_ch: 金人巷，我斯科特的尊严，又回来了！
t_en: Aurum Alley! I, Skott, have returned!

t_ch: 之后，斯科特信守承诺，教三月七学习驾驶机甲。
t_en: And true to his word, Skott teaches March 7th how to pilot a mech.

t_ch: 就这样，三月七成为了银河首屈一指的机甲战斗专家。
t_en: And just like that, March 7th became one of the best mech pilots in the entire Cosmos.

t_ch: 如果再给我一次机会，我一定努力练剑，不会再输了！
t_en: If I get another chance, I'll practice even harder and won't ever lose again!

t_ch: 胜败乃兵家常事，请女侠重新来过。
t_en: Winning and losing are all part of the game, I encourage you to try again young hero.

t_ch: 瞧瞧，这不是我们女侠三月七吗？
t_en: Well, well, if it isn't our hero March 7th!

t_ch: 哼，本姑娘没有躲没有藏，你下了战帖，我就前来应战。
t_en: Hmph, I'm not hiding or backing down. You challenged me, and I'm here to accept it.

t_ch: 你们这些人是不是有毛病啊？比武就比武，还给我写恐吓信，吓唬谁呢？
t_en: We're here to fight. What's wrong with you people? Sending me a threatening letter? Who are you trying to scare?

t_ch: 恐吓信？什么恐吓信啊？
t_en: A threatening letter? What are you even talking about?

t_ch: 少装傻，这不是你们偷偷塞我口袋里的吗？
t_en: Oh, stop playing dumb! Didn't you slip this into my pocket?

t_ch: 「恃强凌弱、傲慢无礼的大罪人，斯科特先生。」
t_en: "Mr. Skott, a great sinner of arrogance and insolence."

t_ch: 「你将扭曲的权力欲望施加在无辜的店主身上，并对拥有历史与传承的仙舟剑术出言不逊。」
t_en: "You have inflicted your twisted lust for power upon that innocent shop owner and insulted the extraordinary art of Xianzhou swordplay."

t_ch: 「我们决定，让你亲口坦白你的一切罪行，你那扭曲的欲望，将由我们取走。」
t_en: "We have decided to make you confess all your crimes in person. The time for change is now, your twisted desires will be stripped away by us."

t_ch: 这字也太难看了…你刚才要没给我读一遍，我都没认出来写的是什么。
t_en: The writing is terrible... I wouldn't have understood it if you hadn't just read it to me.

t_ch: 你找死！
t_en: What the heck did you just say!?

t_ch: 刚才谁说话？
t_en: Hey, who said that?

t_ch: 没…谁都没说话！你听错了！
t_en: No... no one said anything! You must have imagined it!

t_ch: 好了，废话少说，三月七，准备好学狗……
t_en: Well, enough for chit-chat. March 7th, I'm going to make you bark...

t_ch: 我甚至没看到她拔剑，时间好像静止一般，而我只看到一道绿色火光闪过。
t_en: I didn't even see her draw her sword. Time seemed to freeze, and all I saw was a flash of green fire.

t_ch: 刹那间我回想起，儿时我曾在海边凝望彼方，看到浓雾背后有一盏绿灯若隐若现。后来我才知道，那是那个女孩——拉克什米家的房子。
t_en: In an instant, I remembered staring out at the sea as a child, glimpsing a faint green light through the thick fog, which turned out to be the home of that girl — Rakshmi.

t_ch: 什么人会在家里点绿灯啊？他们家真的蛮不正常的。
t_en: Seriously, who lights a green lantern in their house? Those guys must be nuts.

t_ch: 啊…为什么会突然想起这些事？这是走马灯吗？我是不是…已经死了……
t_en: Ah... why am I suddenly remembering these things? Is my life flashing before my eyes? Am I... dead...?

t_ch: 看起来这就是斯科特的内心世界了，老子得找找他最扭曲的欲望是哪里。
t_en: Looks like this is Skott's mind. I'd better find where his most twisted desires are lurking.

t_ch: 斯科特！你赶紧给我滚过来！！！
t_en: Skott! Get your butt over here!!!

t_ch: 啊，这好像是他爹。他们人类好像经常「要用一生治愈童年」，斯科特可能也不例外？
t_en: Oh, this guy looks just like his old man. Humans spend decades trying to heal their childhood traumas, and Skott might be no different.

t_ch: 爸，大周末的喊什么喊？我正睡懒觉呢！
t_en: What's with all the yelling on a weekend, Dad? I was sleeping!

t_ch: 懒鬼，还睡懒觉…跟你说了多少次，信用点会睡懒觉吗？自由市场会睡懒觉吗？看不见的手会睡懒觉吗？算了，这个一会儿再说。
t_en: Sleeping... Heh, how many times do I have to tell you? Do credits sleep? Does the free market sleep? Does the invisible hand sleep? Forget it, we'll discuss this later.

t_ch: 刚才佩法那的父母来家里了。听说佩法那打飞棍球时被撞断了腿，还是你背他去的医院…一定要来拜访一下「儿子挚友的父亲」。
t_en: Pephana's parents came by earlier. They heard about Pephana breaking his leg while playing skydart, and they found out that you carried him to the hospital... so they decided to visit the "father of their son's best friend."

t_ch: 原来斯科特小时候是如此心地善良。
t_en: So, Skott was kind-hearted as a kid.

t_ch: 你这混账！你和佩法那不是争夺飞棍球队队长之位的竞争关系吗？你和自己的竞争对手玩什么朋友游戏？还背他去医院…你还挺会关心人的啊？
t_en: You idiot! Aren't you and Pephana competing for the captain position on the skydart team? Why are you befriending your own rival? And you even carried him to the hospital...? Heh, you're such a caring guy, aren't you?

t_ch: 跟你说了多少次，林登•斯科特，我们斯科特家的家徽是「孤狼」，家训是「没有敌人、没有朋友、只有猎物」。你会背你的猎物去医院吗？
t_en: Lyndon Skott! How many times have I told you that our Skott family crest is the "Lone Wolf," and our family motto is "No enemies. No friends. Only prey." Would you carry your prey to the hospital?

t_ch: 哎…生在这样的家庭里，该说不说，这小斯科特还真可怜。
t_en: Well... little Skott was pretty unfortunate to be born into such a family.

t_ch: 可是…爸爸，他的腿就是我故意撞断的，我若是不送他去医院，别人该怀疑我了……
t_en: But... Dad, I broke his leg on purpose. If I hadn't taken him to the hospital, others would have suspected me...
Wait, what???

t_ch: 啊，原来是这样…对不起，斯科特，是爸爸不分青红皂白就骂了你。爸爸向你道歉。
t_en: Ah, so that's how it is... I'm so sorry, Skott. I shouldn't have scolded you. I apologize.

t_ch: 没关系的，因为我最喜欢爸爸了～
t_en: It's okay Dad, because you're the best.

t_ch: 呃…真是犬父无虎子。看来这里没有「最扭曲的欲望」。
t_en: Umm... I guess the apple doesn't fall far from the tree. It seems his most twisted desires aren't here.

t_ch: 老斯科特，你涉嫌收受巨额贿赂，跟我们走一趟吧。
t_en: Skott Sr., you're suspected of accepting a huge bribe. Now, come with us.

t_ch: 啊，这老东西被抓了。哈哈。
t_en: Ah! The old guy got caught. Haha.

t_ch: 常在河边走，哪有不湿鞋，哈哈哈。我只有一个问题，长官，谁出卖了我？
t_en: Hahaha, I knew this day would eventually come. I only have one question, Captain: Who sold me out?

t_ch: 这是内部检举，恕我们保密。
t_en: An internal tip-off. We're not allowed to disclose any details.

t_ch: 内部检举…这件事涉及的大部分人，要么没动机，要么没证据…会是谁呢？
t_en: An internal tip-off... Most of those involved have neither the motive nor the evidence... Who could it be?

t_ch: 爸爸，是我做的。我出卖了你。
t_en: It was me, Dad. I turned you in.

t_ch: 你…斯科特，你竟然出卖了我！
t_en: You... Skott! You're the one who turned me in!

t_ch: 真是父慈子孝啊！老子要看哭了。
t_en: Oh my, how moving. I think I might just cry...

t_ch: 我真为你感到骄傲！
t_en: I'm so proud of you!

t_ch: …老子服了。
t_en: ...Proud?

t_ch: 爸爸……
t_en: Dad...

t_ch: 踩着我爬到更高的地方吧！斯科特，你是斯科特家的骄傲，是一匹合格的「孤狼」，爸爸以你为傲。
t_en: Use me as a stepping stone to reach greater heights! Lyndon Skott, you are the pride of the Skott family, a true lone wolf, and your dad is so proud of you.

t_ch: 父亲，我谨遵教诲。
t_en: I'll do as you've taught me, Father.

t_ch: 感觉这人的童年已经没救了…他「最扭曲的欲望」根本不在这里。
t_en: Jeez, this guy is simply hopeless... His most twisted desires aren't here at all.

t_ch: 斯科特，我最好的朋友，我要被调职到塔塔里昂了，下班后一起喝杯酒吧。
t_en: Hey, Skott, my best buddy, I'm getting transferred to the Tartarion. Let's grab a drink after work.

t_ch: 这人竟然还有朋友…搞不好这里能找到些让他洗心革面的关键要素。
t_en: This guy actually has friends... Maybe I can find something here that will make him change his ways.

t_ch: 塔塔里昂？那是边境要塞舰啊，你这段时间的工作也没出什么差错啊？
t_en: The Tartarion? That's the frontier fortification vessel, isn't it? You haven't slipped up at work lately, have you?

t_ch: 哈哈，就是你从中作梗，让我被调职的吧？咱们组可没别人有这样的手段了。
t_en: Haha, so it was you who meddled and got me transferred, right? No one else on our team has the skill to pull something like this off.

t_ch: 笑死，我怎么一点都不意外呢。
t_en: Haha, come on! Why am I not surprised at all?

t_ch: …没办法。你那个职位实在太令人垂涎了，我只能这样做。
t_en: ...It couldn't be helped. Your position was so desirable. I had to make a move.

t_ch: 不只是为了职位吧？还有…拉克什米。以后没人会再和你争抢了。
t_en: It's not just about the position, is it? There's also Rakshmi... no one will ever compete with you for her again.

t_ch: 拉克什米…？「浓雾背后有一盏绿灯若隐若现」…难道她是斯科特心心念念的爱人？
t_en: Rakshmi...? "Glimpsing a faint green light through the thick fog..." Could it be that Rakshmi is the love of Skott's life?

t_ch: 请向我保证，你会好好对待她。
t_en: Please promise me that you'll treat her well.

t_ch: 当然了，阿沙瓦特，你就放心地去塔塔里昂赴任吧。我会替你照顾好拉克什米的。
t_en: Of course, Ashavat. Go to the Tartarion without worries. I'll take care of her while you're away.

t_ch: 看来「最扭曲的欲望」也不在这里。
t_en: Seems like his most twisted desires are not here, either.

t_ch: 斯科特，我们认识多久了？
t_en: How long have we known each other, Skott?

t_ch: 7、8年了吧？怎么了？
t_en: Around seven or eight years? Why?

t_ch: 我们一起工作了这么多年，竟然才发现老家离得那么近，哈哈，你说离谱不离谱。
t_en: We've been working together for so long, and I just realized our hometowns are so close to each other. Haha, talk about a coincidence.

t_ch: 我们平时在公司也没机会聊这些嘛。
t_en: Yeah, we never get a chance to talk about this stuff at the office.

t_ch: 没想到斯科特还是个纯情的人渣。
t_en: Oh, I didn't realize Skott was actually a scumbag with a pure heart.

t_ch: 这些年，有句话我一直憋在心里，不敢对你说。
t_en: All these years, there's something I've been keeping to myself, too afraid to tell you.

t_ch: 你喜欢我，对吗？
t_en: You like me, don't you?

t_ch: 你…你怎么这么突然！
t_en: Wh—Why are you being so blunt?

t_ch: 喔～很不错嘛斯科特，很直球！老子很欣赏你。
t_en: Oh! Well done, Skott! I like your straightforward style!

t_ch: 嗯…斯科特，我想和你成为恋人。
t_en: Yeah... I want to be your girlfriend, Skott.

t_ch: 斯科特，终于让老子找到了，这就是你的软肋！
t_en: Hahaha! Gotcha, Skott! I've found your weakness!

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈哈！终于让我抓住了你的软肋！
t_en: Hahaha! Gotcha! Caught you off guard!
Wait, what???

t_ch: 你刚才说的话，我已经全部录音了！咱们部门可是明令禁止办公室恋情的，只要我将这段录音发出去，你就再也没机会和我竞争专员的职位了，哈哈哈哈哈！
t_en: I recorded everything! You know office romances are strictly forbidden in our department. Once I make this recording public, you'll never be able to compete with me for the Specialist position! Hahaha!

t_ch: 逆天。
t_en: What the...

t_ch: 这…哈哈哈，斯科特，我喜欢的正是这样的你。
t_en: Well... Hahaha, alright, that's exactly what I love about you, Skott.

t_ch: 你也逆天。
t_en: What the heck...

t_ch: 我明白了，我会给你让路。但等到有一天，我像这样战胜你的时候，请你接受我的心意。
t_en: I'll step aside for you. But when the day comes that I surpass you in the same manner, I hope you'll acknowledge my intentions.

t_ch: 这就不了吧，因为我啊，是一匹「孤狼」啊。
t_en: Thanks, but no thanks. I'm a lone wolf, remember?

t_ch: 你小子真是油盐不进啊。
t_en: Oh, he's such a jerk.

t_ch: 那是一只狼？不对，那是斯科特自己。
t_en: Is that a wolf? Wait, no, that must be Skott himself.

t_ch: 它身后的王冠，一定就是「最扭曲的欲望」了！
t_en: The crown behind him must be his most twisted desire!

t_ch: 终于深入到灵魂深处了，你这人真是无药可救，老子会带走你那扭曲的欲望。
t_en: Finally, I've found the depths of your hopeless soul. I'll strip away your twisted desires.

t_ch: 你是谁？这是我的私人领地，你是怎么进来的？
t_en: Who are you? This is my place. How did you get in here?

t_ch: 本大爷想来就来，想走就走，还需要你同意？
t_en: I can come and go as I please. Do I need your permission?

t_ch: 让本大爷好好说你两句啊…你说说你，为了上位，你是一点拟人的事都不干啊！
t_en: Well, let me tell you something... In your quest to reach the top, you've trampled over everything that's decent!

t_ch: 这一切都是为了成功。我是斯科特家的骄傲，是一匹合格的「孤狼」，为了走上人生巅峰，我什么都做得出来。
t_en: It's all about success. I'm the pride of the Skott family, a certified "lone wolf." I'll do whatever it takes to reach the pinnacle of life.

t_ch: 可你真的快乐吗？你牺牲了亲情、友情、爱情，只为了那些虚无缥缈的信用点…你好好扪心自问一下，你真的快乐吗？
t_en: But are you truly happy? You've sacrificed your family, your friends, and your love, all for those meaningless credits... Ask yourself: Are you truly happy?

t_ch: 我快乐吗…我…我真的快乐吗…我……
t_en: Am I happy...? Am I... truly happy... I...

t_ch: 我真的太快乐了！我的快乐你根本想象不到！
t_en: Oh! I'm so happy! I'm ecstatic! You couldn't even begin to imagine how happy I am!

t_ch: 「牺牲感情」？天真！「感情」有什么用？「感情」能变成我餐盘里的火腿吗？能变成我酒杯里的红酒吗？能变成我开的星舰、住的豪宅吗？
t_en: Sacrificing my emotions? Hah, what a joke! What good do my emotions do me? Can they turn into a delicious meal or a glass of wine? Can they pilot a starship or buy me a mansion?

t_ch: 「感情」能换几个信用点？啊？你倒是回答我看看啊？但是信用点可以换感情啊，只要信用点足够多，我想让谁做我的朋友，谁就能做我的朋友！
t_en: So, answer me this! Do you think anyone would pay credits for my "emotions"? No way! But I can trade my credits for emotions! With enough credits, I can make anyone my friend!

t_ch: 啊…这人确实没救了。也许要换个思路对付他。
t_en: Ah... this man is truly hopeless. Maybe I need to try a different approach this time.

t_ch: 但我才不会把钱花到「感情」这种毫无意义的事情上！因为我是一匹「孤狼」，一匹斯科特家的「孤狼」！没有敌人、没有朋友、只有猎物！
t_en: But I'm not about to spend my hard-earned credits on something as pointless as emotions! I'm a lone wolf! A lone wolf from the Skott family! With no enemies or friends, only prey!

t_ch: 来吧，「孤狼」斯科特，瞧瞧本大爷的厉害吧！
t_en: Bring it on, Skott the lone wolf! I'll show you what I'm made of!

t_ch: 三月七凭剑术获胜之后，斯科特愿赌服输，道歉认错并离开了金人巷。
t_en: After March 7th achieves victory via her swordsmanship, Skott admits defeat, apologizes, and leaves Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 一段小插曲结束了，而三月七的剑术学习还将继续。
t_en: This brief interlude has concluded, yet March 7th's pursuit of the art of swordplay shall continue.

t_ch: 藿藿将尾巴的封印解除，让他深入斯科特内心，被夺取了扭曲欲望的斯科特改头换面。
t_en: Unsealing Tail, Huohuo makes him enter Skott's inner world. After Skott is relieved of his twisted desire, he turns over a new leaf.

t_ch: 一段小插曲结束了，而三月七的剑术学习还将继续。
t_en: This brief interlude has concluded, yet March 7th's pursuit of the art of swordplay shall continue.

t_ch: 哎…马上就要和斯科特决战了，我怎么觉得自己的武艺还没什么决定性的进步呢！这样真的能打败斯科特吗？担忧！
t_en: ...I'm about to duel with Skott, but I always feel like my fighting skills haven't improved enough! Can I really defeat Skott? I'm starting to worry!

t_ch: 难道没有那种，可以在短时间内打通经脉、提升修为、强身健体的仙丹吗？就是那种，只要吃一颗，立刻能成为绝世邪剑仙的丹药！
t_en: I mean, don't you have some miracle pills that can instantly unlock my meridians, boost my cultivation, and make me a super strong hero? You know, the kind where you just pop one and instantly become an invincible swordmaster!

t_ch: 你是药王秘传吗？
t_en: Are you a disciple of Sanctus Medicus or something?

t_ch: 唉唉唉！当着这几位医士的面可不敢这么乱说，我怕被扭送地衡司！
t_en: Hey! Don't say something like that in front of these healers! I don't want to end up in some Realm-Keeping Commission court!

t_ch: 虽然这个想法很危险，但三月小姐终于学会不择手段赢得胜利了，我心甚慰。
t_en: Well, even though it's a risky idea, I'm glad you're finally willing to do whatever it takes to win, Miss March.

t_ch: 这夸奖我听了一点也不开心！
t_en: Hey, that's actually a compliment I don't want to hear!

t_ch: 怎么可能会有那种丹药啦！
t_en: Come on, do you really think there's a magical pill like that?

t_ch: 怎么不可能？西衍先生的《开拓豪侠传》里不是就有吗？吃下那个「九转升天丹」能暴打幻胧，暴打个斯科特不过分吧？
t_en: I mean why not? Haven't you heard the stories in Mr. Xiyan's Legend of the Trailblazer? The hero took the Elixir of Dragon Oil and gave Phantylia a good beating! Why can't I just do the same to Skott?

t_ch: 《开拓豪侠传》写得靠不靠谱，难道你自己不知道吗？
t_en: Do you even realize how much those legends get exaggerated?

t_ch: 仙舟上可没有那么方便的药物啦。不过如果只是为了在赛前让自己的体力短暂提升，那也不是做不到。
t_en: We don't have any convenient pills like that, but if you just need a temporary boost for the duel, we might be able to figure something out.

t_ch: 三月小姐，我给你熬了一碗「松茸龙眼山白竹雀汤」，又滋养又大补，吃完以后保准你浑身都是劲儿。
t_en: I've made a bowl of Matsutake Longan White Bamboo Sparrow Soup for you, Miss March. It's nourishing and will give you a good energy boost.

t_ch: 我也要喝！我也要喝！
t_en: I want some too! I want some too!

t_ch: 去去去，你别凑热闹！这药膳是大补之物，若稍后没有大运动量消耗，反而对身体有害。
t_en: Hey now! This tonic is very potent, if you don't engage in any intense physical activity, it might do more harm than good.

t_ch: 赛前灌个水饱真的好吗？
t_en: Is it really a good idea to drink a lot of liquid before a fight?

t_ch: 放心吧，身体会提醒你速战速决的～
t_en: Ah don't worry, your body will remind you to finish the fight quickly.

t_ch: 那我能不能先喝一半，剩下一半？万一被斯科特打败了，拿来补身体……
t_en: Can I drink half now and save the other half for later? Just in case Skott beats me, I can use it to recover...

t_ch: 三月小姐这么缺乏自信吗？唔…我本来还等着给公司的人治伤看诊，收三倍诊金呢。
t_en: Are you really that lacking in confidence, March? Hmm... I was actually looking forward to tripling the fee when the IPC guys came in for treatment.

t_ch: 小神医你是不知道，那公司的机甲，打起人来可疼了！
t_en: You don't know the half of it! Those IPC mechs pack a huge punch!

t_ch: 只是怕疼，妾身倒是有办法……
t_en: If it's just pain you're afraid of, I might have a solution...

t_ch: 此药名唤「押不芦」，只消吃上三钱，就能屏蔽人体感官，让人不觉疼痛。
t_en: Here, this medicine is named "Yabruh." A tiny dose can numb the body's senses and alleviate any sensation of pain.

t_ch: 云骑军某些丹药中也有微量此成分。战阵之上，人人都难免因疼痛而怯懦，所以只要屏蔽疼痛，那自然胜券在握。
t_en: Actually, some of the Cloud Knights use pellets which contain trace amounts of this ingredient. When you block pain on the battlefield, you have a better chance of beating your opponent and surviving.

t_ch: 哇～还有这种神药！但…不会有什么副作用吧？
t_en: Whoa! Sounds incredible! But... are there any side effects?

t_ch: 那不会。丹鼎司做手术都用它，能有什么副作用？只不过……
t_en: No, not at all. We use it for surgeries in the Alchemy Commission, so what kind of side effects could it possibly have? It's just...

t_ch: 只不过？
t_en: Just what?

t_ch: 只不过，痛觉是人体的警告信号，无视痛觉，一意孤行战斗下去…那很有可能，受了致命伤却不自知啊。
t_en: Well, the thing is, pain is your body's way of warning you something is wrong. If you ignore it and push through... you might end up with some serious injuries without even realizing it.

t_ch: 妾身曾接诊过一名患者，此人与人决斗前服用该药，打完了架才发现…他已经只剩下上半身了……
t_en: I had a patient who took some before a fight, only to realize that afterwards... his lower body was missing...

t_ch: 噫！快别说了，我不吃了，疼就疼吧！
t_en: Whew! Okay, enough! I'm not taking any medicine. I'll just tough it out!

t_ch: 我们都在出主意想办法，怎么只有椒丘大夫一直都不说话？
t_en: We're all brainstorming ideas, why are you so quiet, Doctor Jiaoqiu?

t_ch: 椒丘大夫，椒丘！三月小姐马上就要去和斯科特决战了，你在干什么呢！
t_en: Doctor Jiaoqiu? Jiaoqiu! March is about to face off with Skott! What are you doing?

t_ch: 啊，不好意思。刚听闻三月小姐准备靠「丹药」解决斯科特，就稍微做了些安排。
t_en: Oh, sorry! I heard March was planning to use some medicine against Skott, so I made some preparations.

t_ch: 唉，诸位的医道理论仍然不尽完善啊。战斗能力提升是个相对概念，其实只要敌弱我强就行，请诸位将思路打开。
t_en: Well, it seems you're struggling to think outside the box. Sometimes, weakening your opponent is just as effective as improving your own combat skills. It's all about innovation.

t_ch: 莫非曜青的医术里有什么秘方能助我一臂之力？
t_en: Wait, could it be that the Yaoqing people have some secret formula that could help me?

t_ch: 没错，这是曜青「染指派」医食同源之术的秘中之秘！若不是为三月小姐出战，我是绝不外传的。
t_en: Exactly! This is the closely guarded secret of the Yaoqing's Ranzhi School. If it weren't for Miss March's upcoming battle, I would never divulge it.

t_ch: 你…难道你准备给斯科特下泻药？？？
t_en: Wait... do you plan on slipping Skott a laxative?

t_ch: …你是怎么知道的？哼，鄙人的城府还是不够深啊。
t_en: ...How did you figure that out? Hmph, looks like I'm too predictable.

t_ch: 太伟大了，仙舟医学。
t_en: Praise be to the Xianzhou medicine.

t_ch: 别把这家伙和我们相提并论。
t_en: Seriously, don't even compare this guy to us.

t_ch: 太卑鄙了，「鄙人」医生。
t_en: How despicable, Doc "Despicable."

t_ch: 你们称之为「卑鄙」，而我称之为「高效」。
t_en: What you call "despicable," I call "efficient."

t_ch: 那个…椒丘大夫，这是不是不太好？我们是不是应该堂堂正正一点…要不还是别这么做了吧？
t_en: Well... Doctor Jiaoqiu? Are we really doing this? Shouldn't we defeat him in a more honorable way...? Maybe we should just completely forget about this idea.

t_ch: 啊？你不同意啊？可是现在已经晚了，貊泽已经把事情办妥了。
t_en: Huh? You don't agree with my methods? Hmm, but it's already too late. Moze has already taken care of things.

t_ch: 难道你刚才低头玩手机就是在安排貊泽…你们曜青人行动力都这么强的吗！
t_en: Wait, so while you seemed to be playing with your phone, you were actually arranging things with Moze? Are all Yaoqing people as quick as you?

t_ch: 诶呀，已经到决战的时间了，快去和斯科特先生决一死战吧！我还有事，先走一步。
t_en: Alright, it's time for the duel! Just go fight that Skott guy! I've got something else to do, so I'll be off.

t_ch: 他跑了！啊这这这，这怎么办……
t_en: He ran away! Ah... Wh—What should I do now...

t_ch: 木已成舟，三月小姐，只能上了。
t_en: What's done is done, March. We just have to keep moving forward.

t_ch: 别担心，我们会抢救斯科特的。希望人没事。
t_en: Rest assured, we will come to Skott's aid if needed. Let's hope he's alright.

t_ch: 走吧…{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Let's go... {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 三月七，你终于来了！你都不知道吧，你在这金人巷已经非常不得人心了！
t_en: Ah, March 7th, you've finally decided to show up! Bet you don't know how incredibly unpopular you are here in Aurum Alley!

t_ch: 不得人心？你说什么呢，这金人巷里谁不喜欢我活泼可爱的小三月？
t_en: Unpopular? What are you talking about? Who in Aurum Alley wouldn't like a cheerful and adorable young lady like me?

t_ch: 哈哈，我都听人说了，他们只是慑于彦卿和云璃的淫威，敢怒不敢言罢了！
t_en: Oh, I've caught wind of the gossip. They're just scared of Yanqing and Yunli's power, so they don't have the guts to speak up!

t_ch: 我告诉你，刚才有一位勇士偷偷找到我，鼓励我勇敢战胜你，为金人巷的乡亲们狠狠出一口恶气。
t_en: Guess what? A brave soul approached me in secret earlier, encouraging me to defeat you and seek revenge for all the people in Aurum Alley!

t_ch: 不仅如此，他还送了我和兄弟们一大锅药膳汤，让我们喝完药膳，再精神百倍地对付你。
t_en: And you know what else? He gave me and the guys a big pot of this special medicinal soup to drink so we can come at you with triple the energy.

t_ch: 哈哈，那还真是…古道热肠的勇士啊……
t_en: Haha, well... they do sound like... a brave soul...

t_ch: 那你们喝完了吗？
t_en: So, did you guys take it all?

t_ch: 当然喝完了。那药膳汤真是美味极了！没想到在人心冷漠的罗浮，还能喝到这样滚热肠胃的汤，真是出人意料啊。
t_en: Of course we did! That soup was surprisingly delicious. Who'd have thought, in a cold and indifferent place like the Luofu, we'd find something so heartwarming?

t_ch: 那个汤里有泻药。
t_en: There was a laxative in that soup.

t_ch: 你当我不知道吗？你说这话就是想分化瓦解我们和金人巷有识之士之间因共同利益和阶级感情所结成的攻守同盟并以此达成击败我的目的。
t_en: You think I'm an idiot? Your statement aims to dismantle and fragment the coalition established on the basis of shared interests and class affinities between ourselves and the erudite individuals dwelling in Aurum Alley, with the ultimate goal of defeating me.

t_ch: 你这长难句我愣是没听懂。
t_en: Um... I didn't understand a word of what you just said.

t_ch: 行了，废话说够多了。兄弟们，上吧！让百姓们悉心烹饪的药膳汤带给我们更多力量！绝不能输！
t_en: Alright, enough talking! Let's go, boys! That delicious medicinal soup will give us the strength to win!

t_ch: 你们几个别太逞强啊！撑不住了就赶紧撤！
t_en: Don't push yourself too hard! Just leave if you can't handle it!

t_ch: 不用你操心！！！
t_en: Spare me your fake concern!!!

t_ch: 我输了。我输了。三月七，你太卑鄙了。
t_en: I lost. I am defeated... You fight too dirty, March 7th. You are truly despicable.

t_ch: 你还是快去卫生间吧……
t_en: You'd better go to the bathroom...

t_ch: 不，已经不用去了。
t_en: No... I don't need to go to the bathroom anymore.

t_ch: 学猪叫环节呢？
t_en: What about the "pig squealing" part?

t_ch: 他都这样了，就算了吧。
t_en: Let's just forget about that part. He's already in such a sorry state.

t_ch: 我们确实有点卑鄙了。
t_en: I guess we did fight a bit too dirty.

t_ch: …但内心深处有点痛快。这是不是堕入黑暗面的开始？
t_en: ...But deep down, it feels kinda good. Is this what slipping into the dark side feels like?

t_ch: 三月七…我还有最后一句话。
t_en: I have one last thing to say... March 7th.

t_ch: 你要是想骂我的话就骂吧！这一招我也觉得有点过分……
t_en: Fine, give me a piece of your mind if you want! I admit it. I was kinda out of line...

t_ch: 我认可你了。
t_en: You've earned my respect.
Wait, what???

t_ch: 三月七，你是我认识的第一个，和我一样卑鄙无耻、残酷无情、不择手段的人。这一次，是你更卑鄙无耻、更残酷无情，更不择手段！我输得心服口服！
t_en: March 7th, you're the first person I've ever known who's just as despicable, cruel, and as heartless as me. But this time, you outdid yourself, being even more vile, more ruthless, and more unscrupulous than ever! I accept my defeat!

t_ch: 也许有一天，我们会成为真正的朋友。如果你想来公司工作，我也一定会为你写推荐信。
t_en: Maybe we'll actually become real friends someday. If you want to come and work for the IPC, I'll gladly write you a recommendation letter.

t_ch: 听你夸奖完，我真的好生气。{NICKNAME}，我可以趁他站不起来，再打他一顿吗？
t_en: Your compliments make me so mad. Can I give him another beating while he's down, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 动手吧。
t_en: Go for it.

t_ch: 动手吧，毕竟，我已经没有还手之力了。
t_en: Go for it. I don't have the strength to fight back anyway.

t_ch: 一滴冰冷的泪珠，终于裹挟着强忍不住的悲伤，从男人的眼角，滑落。
t_en: A single drop of icy tear, finally succumbing to uncontainable sorrow, escapes from the corner of the man's eye.

t_ch: 算了，我不会乘人之危的。
t_en: Forget it, I don't kick people when they're down, even if they annoy me.

t_ch: 放过他吧。
t_en: Spare him.

t_ch: 放过我吧。毕竟，我已经没有还手之力了。
t_en: Spare me, please. I don't have the strength to fight back.

t_ch: 一滴冰冷的泪珠，终于裹挟着强忍不住的悲伤，从男人的眼角，滑落。
t_en: A single drop of icy tear, finally succumbing to uncontainable sorrow, escapes from the corner of the man's eye.

t_ch: 不行，不能放过。因为我很卑鄙。
t_en: No way, I won't let him off the hook, because I'm despicable, remember?

t_ch: 弟兄们！弟兄们！还有能动的弟兄吗！快来救我！
t_en: Guys! Fellas! Anyone else who can still move! Help me!

t_ch: 几个尚能活动的公司员工冲进人群，七手八脚地抬走了斯科特专员。
t_en: Several IPC workers who can still move rush into the crowd and carry off Skott.

t_ch: 三月小姐…下次还是用些堂堂正正的办法吧。
t_en: Miss March... maybe we should try something more honorable next time.

t_ch: 不是我的主意！是那个粉毛狐狸自作主张的！我这最多只能算是…算是…无限制剑斗流？
t_en: It wasn't my idea! It was all that pink-haired fox acting on his own! All I did was... well... kind of... engage in free-form sword fighting?

t_ch: 一段小插曲结束了，而三月七的剑术学习还将继续。
t_en: This brief interlude has concluded, yet March 7th's pursuit of the art of swordplay shall continue.

t_ch: 嗯？三月小姐这是在写信吗？
t_en: Hmm? Are you writing a letter, Miss March?

t_ch: 是呀～出来这么久了，也该给星穹列车报个平安才对。
t_en: Yeah, it's been a while, and I thought I should let the Express know that I'm safe and sound here.

t_ch: 那你可要好好写写咱们这次辉煌的胜利！
t_en: You should also tell them about our glorious victory!

t_ch: 呃啊…这个，还是不要告诉他们了吧，哈哈哈。
t_en: Well... maybe it's better not to mention it... Haha.

t_ch: 姬子、杨叔、帕姆：见信如晤。我们在罗浮一切都好，请各位放心。你们的旅行还顺利吧？
t_en: Dear Himeko, Mr. Yang, and Pom-Pom, We're all good here on the Xianzhou Luofu, so no need to worry! By the way, how's your trip going?

t_ch: 椒丘派遣貊泽给斯科特一行人猛下泻药，你们不费吹灰之力取得了剑斗的胜利。
t_en: Jiaoqiu dispatches Moze to administer a strong laxative to Skott and his group, allowing you to effortlessly secure victory in the sword duel.

t_ch: 一段小插曲结束了，而三月七的剑术学习还将继续。
t_en: This brief interlude has concluded, yet March 7th's pursuit of the art of swordplay shall continue.

t_ch: 呃啊…肚子好疼…怎么回事…
t_en: Uhhh... my stomach hurts... What's happening...?

t_ch: 我…我肚子也疼，难道那碗药膳真的有问题？
t_en: M—Mine hurts too! Could it be that... something was wrong with that soup?

t_ch: 卑鄙的仙舟人！
t_en: Those vile Xianzhou scoundrels!

t_ch: 总之，坚持一下！先打倒这个小妮子再说！
t_en: Just hold on, boys! Let's defeat this little brat first!

t_ch: 不行了，我感觉自己昨天的晚餐要离我而去！
t_en: Not good. I think last night's dinner is about to make a grand exit!

t_ch: 去…去个卫生间！再见！
t_en: I... gotta go to the bathroom! See you!

t_ch: 混账，不许跑！
t_en: Stop there! Don't you dare run away!

t_ch: 对不起！我也撑不住了，真的要、要、要…
t_en: Sorry, but I can't hold it any longer! I really... really...

t_ch: 好了不要再说下去了！快滚！滚啊！
t_en: Spare me the details! Just get out of here!

t_ch: 好疼…眼前出现了幻觉…
t_en: So painful... I'm seeing things...

t_ch: 星穹列车正在向外…奔跑…
t_en: The Astral Express is... racing away...

t_ch: 可恶啊！可恶的星穹列车！不许发车！
t_en: Cursed Astral Express! You're not allowed to leave!

t_ch: 马上就要和斯科特对决了，真是紧张。
t_en: I'm going up against Skott soon. I'm so nervous.

t_ch: 紧张什么？对付几个公司的杂鱼，很简单的。
t_en: Nervous about what? Dealing with a few IPC minions is just a walk in the park.

t_ch: 可是，到了比武的时候，斯科特肯定会用上他们公司的机甲。那东西怎么想也不是能用两把剑解决掉的吧！
t_en: But Skott will definitely use the IPC's mechs. There's no way I can take them down with just a couple of swords, right?

t_ch: 彦卿和云璃肯定可以。
t_en: It wouldn't be a problem for Yanqing or Yunli.

t_ch: 师父们肯定可以啊！但和他们比，是不是太为难我人见人欺的小三月了？
t_en: Masters could handle them for sure! But isn't it asking too much of a poor little rookie like me?

t_ch: 那东西的装甲太厚了。
t_en: The mechs' armor is too thick.

t_ch: 对吧？就算彦卿师父说这剑器里暗藏有助于切割的机关…但它真的能切开敌人的机甲吗？
t_en: Right? Even if Master Yanqing says there's a mechanism in this sword that helps with cutting... can it really slice through a mech?

t_ch: 为什么要执着于物理破坏呢？对黑客来说，最理想的战斗是从内部攻破对手。
t_en: Why focus on piercing the armor? For hackers, the ideal way to win is to infiltrate the opponent's system.

t_ch: 来，把你的剑给我。
t_en: Here, give me your sword.

t_ch: 没人知道银狼到底对三月七的剑做了什么。
t_en: No one has any idea what Silver Wolf did to March 7th's sword.

t_ch: 行啊，你被强化了，上吧。
t_en: Alright, you've been enhanced. Go for it.

t_ch: 你做了什么？
t_en: What did you do?

t_ch: 等到比武的时候你们就明白了。
t_en: You'll understand once the duel begins.

t_ch: 另一把剑不用强化吗？
t_en: Don't you need to enhance the other sword too?

t_ch: 编程讲究少即是多，做太多事反而容易出纰漏。
t_en: No, when it comes to programming, less is more. Overdoing it increases the chance of creating errors.

t_ch: 这样…就可以打败斯科特了？
t_en: So... I can defeat him just like that?

t_ch: 哼哼～很喜欢仙舟人的一句俗语：「知己知彼，对手狗殆。」
t_en: Yeah, there's a Xianzhou proverb for such an occasion: "Know yourself and know your enemy, and you'll never have to sleep in the doghouse."

t_ch: 后半句是你自己乱编的吧……
t_en: You made up the second half, didn't you...?

t_ch: 你懂什么，这叫狗尾续…这叫继往开来！好了，现在不是讨论仙舟俗语的时候，快去让斯科特尝尝你的厉害吧。
t_en: What do you know? Just let sleeping dogs lie! Anyway, now's not the time for discussing Xianzhou sayings. You've got bigger fish to fry. Go and show Skott what you're made of.

t_ch: 三月七，你来晚了。我还以为你不敢来了，正发愁该怎么找你算账呢～
t_en: March 7th, you're late. Here I was thinking you were too chicken to show your face. I was thinking I might have to track you down and settle the score~

t_ch: 我那是不敢来吗？我那是给你一个反悔的机会！没想到你不但没逃走，竟然还敢在这里叫嚣！
t_en: Too chicken? I was giving you the chance to back out! But you're actually insulting me now, you cheeky...

t_ch: 我们是列车，难免晚点。
t_en: We ride a train everywhere, delays are to be expected every once in a while.

t_ch: 能把迟到说得这么理直气壮，要是在我手底下干活，我一定扒你一层皮。
t_en: Making excuses for being late like it's no big deal? If you worked under me, I'd personally make you regret it.

t_ch: 那么着急比武，活不到明天了？
t_en: What's the rush? You only got one day left to live or something?

t_ch: 呵，希望你们手上的功夫和嘴上的功夫一样强。
t_en: Heh, I hope your actions are as strong as that mouth of yours.

t_ch: 也不知道银狼到底在我的剑上动了什么手脚，真能赢过斯科特吗？
t_en: I wonder what Silver Wolf did to my sword. Can I really defeat Skott?

t_ch: 嘿，尊敬的三月七小姐，请放心，会赢赢的。
t_en: Don't worry, dear Miss March! Rest assured, we will win.

t_ch: 呜哇！我的剑说话了！
t_en: Whoa! My sword is talking!

t_ch: 尊敬的三月七小姐，我是战术人工智能普罗米括弧{RUBY_B#未付费破解版}学习版{RUBY_E#}括弧，我将协助您战胜敌人。
t_en: Greetings, Miss March 7th. I am the Tactical AI, Prome, *parenthesis* {RUBY_B#Unpaid Cracked Version}Learning Edition{RUBY_E#} *close parenthesis*. My purpose is to aid you in overcoming adversaries.

t_ch: 你能提高武器性能？
t_en: Can you enhance weapons?

t_ch: 不能，恰恰相反，在将我接入您的武器时，银狼粗糙的焊接技能使得剑体的抗冲击性能下降了百分之十二。
t_en: Quite the opposite, actually. When Silver Wolf installed me into your sword, her terrible welding skills reduced the sword's impact resistance by <unbreak>12</unbreak> percent.

t_ch: 但是，请别担心，我能帮助您获得胜利。
t_en: But please do not worry. I'll help you secure victory.

t_ch: 银狼真的好厉害…
t_en: Silver Wolf is simply amazing...
